name: latency_spike
title: Cross-Venue Latency Spike
description: All FIX engine connections and market data feeds experience 500ms+ latency, causing heartbeat timeouts and stale order state.
background: A network infrastructure event (e.g., DDoS on upstream ISP, BGP route flap, or datacenter switch failover) injects latency across all connections. FIX heartbeat intervals (typically 30s) start timing out when round-trip exceeds the tolerance window. Market data becomes stale — quotes are 500ms+ old, which is catastrophic for algo trading. The broker must detect the systemic latency, pause algo slicing, flag stale quotes, and potentially circuit-break all new order routing until the issue resolves.

steps.action: Establish baseline latency across all exchange connections (<5ms typical)
steps.expected_state: All engines report <10ms latency; heartbeats flowing normally

steps.action: Inject 500ms latency across all connections simultaneously
steps.expected_state: Latency metrics spike; heartbeat age starts climbing

steps.action: Monitor for heartbeat timeout warnings on any engine
steps.expected_state: Engines with 30s heartbeat interval show warnings after ~30-60s

steps.action: Check market data freshness — all venues should show stale
steps.expected_state: Market data freshness > 500ms; quotes flagged as stale

steps.action: Verify algo orders pause or flag stale data warnings
steps.expected_state: Active algos either pause slicing or flag STALE_QUOTE warning

steps.action: Clear latency injection and verify full recovery
steps.expected_state: Latency returns to <10ms; heartbeats resume; algos resume slicing
